records.ety: [OE. henge, heeng; akin to D. heng, LG. henge, Prov. E. hingle a small hinge; connected with hang, v., and Icel. hengja to hang. See Hang.]

defs: The hook with its eye, or the joint, on which a door, gate, lid, etc., turns or swings; a flexible piece, as a strip of leather, which serves as a joint to turn on.
quotes: The gate self-opened wide, On golden hinges turning.

defs: That on which anything turns or depends; a governing principle; a cardinal point or rule; as, this argument was the hinge on which the question turned.
One of the four cardinal points, east, west, north, or south.
quotes: When the moon is in the hinge at East.
Nor slept the winds . . . but rushed abroad.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Hinged (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Hinging (?).]

defs: To attach by, or furnish with, hinges.
To stand, depend, hang, or turn, as on a hinge; to depend chiefly for a result or decision or for force and validity; -- usually with on or upon; as, the argument hinges on this point.
